title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new recipe box subscription they’ve tried.
extracts.audio_script: Man: So, did you manage to cook that sea bass recipe from the 'Chef-Box'? It looked quite fancy in the photo. || Woman: It was delicious. But [1]I found the estimated prep time on the recipe card to be wildly optimistic[/1]. It said fifteen minutes, but I was still chopping veg half an hour later. || Man: I had the same issue with the risotto! I think they assume we all have professional knife skills. || Woman: [2]What I really appreciated, though, was the lack of food waste[/2]. It was so satisfying only having exactly the amount of spices I actually needed for the dish. || Man: True, it beats having six half-empty jars of obscure herbs at the back of the cupboard. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a bit pricey, but for a special occasion, it’s worth the convenience.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So, did you manage to cook that sea bass recipe from the 'Chef-Box'? It looked quite fancy in the photo.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It was delicious. But I found the estimated prep time on the recipe card to be wildly optimistic. It said fifteen minutes, but I was still chopping veg half an hour later.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I had the same issue with the risotto! I think they assume we all have professional knife skills.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What I really appreciated, though, was the lack of food waste. It was so satisfying only having exactly the amount of spices I actually needed for the dish.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: True, it beats having six half-empty jars of obscure herbs at the back of the cupboard.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a bit pricey, but for a special occasion, it’s worth the convenience.

questions.question: What did the woman find frustrating about the recipe box?

options.A: the difficulty of the cooking techniques
options.B: the inaccuracy of the timing instructions
options.C: the poor quality of the fresh ingredients

questions.explanation: She found the estimated prep time to be 'wildly optimistic' and much shorter than reality [1].

questions.question: Both speakers liked the service because it

options.B: was more affordable than eating out.
options.C: prevented the accumulation of unused items.

questions.explanation: They both highlight the benefit of not having 'food waste' or 'half-empty jars' [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a city's new cycle-lane infrastructure.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I finally tried the new segregated cycle path to the office today. It’s a bit of a detour, but I felt much safer. || Woman: [3]I suspect the council is only doing it to meet their green targets[/3], rather than actually listening to what local commuters need. || Man: Why do you say that? It seems like a step in the right direction. || Woman: [4]The way the lanes just abruptly end at the busiest roundabouts is a major safety flaw[/4]. It’s as if they ran out of paint halfway through the project. || Man: I agree, it is a bit of a shock when you’re suddenly thrown back into traffic. || Woman: Exactly. It needs to be a continuous network, not just a few disjointed stretches of blue tarmac. || Man: Let’s hope they finish the rest of it soon.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I finally tried the new segregated cycle path to the office today. It’s a bit of a detour, but I felt much safer.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect the council is only doing it to meet their green targets, rather than actually listening to what local commuters need.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Why do you say that? It seems like a step in the right direction.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: The way the lanes just abruptly end at the busiest roundabouts is a major safety flaw. It’s as if they ran out of paint halfway through the project.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree, it is a bit of a shock when you’re suddenly thrown back into traffic.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It needs to be a continuous network, not just a few disjointed stretches of blue tarmac.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Let’s hope they finish the rest of it soon.

questions.question: What is the woman's opinion of the council's motivation?

options.A: They are trying to reduce traffic congestion.
options.B: They are prioritizing political goals over practical ones.
options.C: They are responding to pressure from local cyclists.

questions.explanation: She suspects they are doing it 'to meet their green targets' rather than helping commuters [3].

questions.question: What is the main problem identifyed with the new lanes?

options.A: They are not wide enough for cargo bikes.
options.B: They are poorly lit at night.

questions.explanation: She notes that the lanes 'abruptly end' and are 'disjointed' [4].

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new museum of modern art.
extracts.audio_script: Man: That exhibition on abstract expressionism was quite a challenge! I’m still not sure what that pile of bricks was supposed to represent. || Woman: [5]I found the lack of descriptive labels near the artworks quite pretentious[/5]. Not all of us have a degree in art history. || Man: I agree. A bit of context would have helped me appreciate it more. || Woman: [6]What really stood out, though, was the architecture of the building itself[/6]. That spiral staircase in the atrium is a masterpiece. || Man: It was stunning. I think I spent more time taking photos of the ceiling than the actual paintings. || Woman: It’s a shame when the building outshines the collection, but it’s definitely worth the visit just for the views from the roof terrace.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That exhibition on abstract expressionism was quite a challenge! I’m still not sure what that pile of bricks was supposed to represent.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the lack of descriptive labels near the artworks quite pretentious. Not all of us have a degree in art history.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. A bit of context would have helped me appreciate it more.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What really stood out, though, was the architecture of the building itself. That spiral staircase in the atrium is a masterpiece.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It was stunning. I think I spent more time taking photos of the ceiling than the actual paintings.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It’s a shame when the building outshines the collection, but it’s definitely worth the visit just for the views from the roof terrace.

questions.question: The woman suggests that the museum

options.A: is too small for the collection.
options.B: lacks sufficient information for the public.
options.C: is overpriced for a local attraction.

questions.explanation: She finds the 'lack of descriptive labels' pretentious and unhelpful [5].

questions.question: Both speakers were particularly impressed by

options.B: the expertise of the museum guides.
options.C: the physical design of the venue.

questions.explanation: They both praise the 'architecture of the building' and the 'spiral staircase' [6].
